jokes.text: From up here, an entire world can seem utterly insignificant.
jokes.setup: Philosophical observation about perspective from space
jokes.punchline: The irony that they're about to visit a world that treats them as insignificant
jokes.explanation: Subtle ironic foreshadowing that works better on rewatch when you know the planet's attitude toward humans

jokes.text: We're close enough that when you hit a player with a bottle, he's down.
jokes.setup: Commenting on good seats at a sporting event
jokes.punchline: Casually suggesting violence against players as a measure of seat quality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender character moment - casual violence delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Baseball? God forbid. Face it, Fry. Baseball was as boring as mom and apple pie.
jokes.setup: Fry asking if blernsball is the same as baseball
jokes.punchline: Subverting the idiom 'as American as mom and apple pie' by calling those things boring too
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that subverts a familiar idiom by extending the comparison

jokes.text: It's tradition, like aluminum bats and the seventh-inning grope.
jokes.setup: Fry asking about the ball on a spring tradition
jokes.punchline: Parody of 'seventh-inning stretch' with inappropriate sexual reference
jokes.explanation: Strong wordplay joke that efficiently parodies baseball traditions with dark humor

jokes.text: I'd like a jumbo squid log, please. / We don't sell those. / All right. Let me have one of your young on a roll. / We're out of rolls. / Fine. Just give me something crawling with parasites.
jokes.setup: Bender trying to order concession food
jokes.punchline: Increasingly disgusting requests escalating to parasites as Bender gets frustrated
jokes.explanation: Great escalating sequence with perfect pacing and increasingly gross requests

jokes.text: At least hot dogs didn't change.
jokes.setup: After all the gross future food options
jokes.punchline: Fry's relief about hot dogs, implying they were already gross to begin with
jokes.explanation: Self-aware joke about hot dog quality that lands with good timing

jokes.text: Anyone want butter on that?
jokes.setup: Bender offering popcorn after popping sounds
jokes.punchline: Implication that Bender literally popped the popcorn from his body, now offering butter
jokes.explanation: Solid robot gag about Bender's body functions as kitchen appliances

jokes.text: I'm getting the hang of this. The blerns are loaded, count's 3 blerns, 2 anti-blerns, and the blern rule's in effect, right? / Except for the word 'blern,' that's total gibberish.
jokes.setup: Fry confidently explaining the complex rules of blernsball
jokes.punchline: Leela pointing out he's just using made-up terms with no actual understanding
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup/payoff about trying to understand incomprehensible rules - very relatable

jokes.text: And Johnson played back in the days before steroids were mandatory.
jokes.setup: Discussing old baseball players and their natural talent
jokes.punchline: Casual mention that steroids are now mandatory in sports
jokes.explanation: Dark sports satire delivered with perfect casualness

jokes.text: Exactly. He was a machine designed to hit blerns. / Wireless Joe was a programmable bat on wheels.
jokes.setup: Discussing legendary robot player Wireless Joe Jackson
jokes.punchline: Reveal that the 'player' was literally just a bat on wheels
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist escalation that subverts expectations about robot athletes

jokes.text: And I suppose pitching at 5000 was just a modified howitzer?
jokes.setup: Following the revelation about the bat on wheels
jokes.punchline: Logical extension of robot sports equipment being literal weapons
jokes.explanation: Good callback escalation that extends the absurd robot sports logic

jokes.text: But how many robot managers are there? -Eleven? -Zero!
jokes.setup: Bender making point about robot discrimination in baseball
jokes.punchline: Fry's optimistic guess is completely wrong
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective - Fry's naive optimism meets harsh reality

jokes.text: Is it a human child? I wish!
jokes.setup: Seeing someone cleaning up filth at the stadium
jokes.punchline: Bender's preference for child labor over robot exploitation
jokes.explanation: Dark character comedy that perfectly captures Bender's twisted priorities

jokes.text: Get away, you filthy bird. / Professor? Turn me off, quick! / I'm sorry. What?
jokes.setup: Bender being attacked by a bird, asking to be turned off
jokes.punchline: Professor's polite confusion at the unusual request
jokes.explanation: Solid physical comedy with good character interplay

jokes.text: Well, aren't they? / I've never made anyone's life easier, and you know it!
jokes.setup: Leela suggesting robots are built to make life easier
jokes.punchline: Bender's proud assertion of his complete uselessness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender character moment - he's proud of being completely unhelpful

jokes.text: Great news, everyone. You'll be delivering a package to Chapek 9. / Where humans are killed on sight. / Why is that great news?
jokes.setup: Professor's typical 'great news' announcement about a delivery
jokes.punchline: The casual mention of human extermination as part of the job
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor Farnsworth misdirection with perfect timing

jokes.text: Just because a robot wants to kill humans, he's a 'radical.'
jokes.setup: Professor describing robot separatists as murderous radicals
jokes.punchline: Bender's casual defense of genocide as normal robot behavior
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Bender's warped perspective on violence

jokes.text: This is the first actual work you've ever done here. / Well, I'm not doing it! It's a robot holiday.
jokes.setup: Pointing out Bender never works
jokes.punchline: Bender immediately finding an excuse to avoid his first assignment
jokes.explanation: Typical Bender behavior - avoiding work with increasingly elaborate excuses

jokes.text: Really? Which one? / Only Robanukah. The holiest two weeks on the robot calendar.
jokes.setup: Leela challenging Bender's robot holiday claim
jokes.punchline: Made-up robot version of Hanukkah lasting an absurd two weeks
jokes.explanation: Good portmanteau joke that establishes Bender's pattern of making up holidays

jokes.text: Come on, Bender. Last month, it was Robomadom. / And before that, Robonza.
jokes.setup: Amy calling out Bender's pattern of fake holidays
jokes.punchline: More made-up robot holidays with religious portmanteaus
jokes.explanation: Good establishment of running gag with religious holiday parodies

jokes.text: Not just a blast, it was a sacred tribute to my ancestral prototypes which took the form of a drinking contest.
jokes.setup: Defending the sacredness of Robonza
jokes.punchline: Religious ceremony that's just an excuse to drink
jokes.explanation: Great Bender logic - turning hedonistic behavior into religious observance

jokes.text: But you used all your days off when you had Roberculosis.
jokes.setup: Hermes pointing out Bender's previous fake illness
jokes.punchline: Robot version of tuberculosis as another made-up excuse
jokes.explanation: Continues the pattern of robot excuse portmanteaus, though less inventive

jokes.text: Kind of like how a warehouse is inhabited by boxes.
jokes.setup: Professor saying the planet is inhabited by robots
jokes.punchline: Leela's casual dehumanization comparing robots to inanimate objects
jokes.explanation: Sharp analogy that efficiently establishes the crew's casual robot prejudice

jokes.text: Yes, Ms. Leela. Tote that space barge. Lift that space bale.
jokes.setup: Being told to get to work
jokes.punchline: Bender's sarcastic reference to slave songs while complaining about his first job
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bender's dramatic self-pity over minimal work

jokes.text: I'll try to keep that in mind.
jokes.setup: Being told he doesn't like humans
jokes.punchline: Bender's casual response to instructions about hiding his friendship with humans
jokes.explanation: Subtle irony as Bender treats serious instructions casually

jokes.text: If only I had two or three minutes to think.
jokes.setup: Major crisis requiring immediate rescue mission
jokes.punchline: Leela's absurdly short timeframe for planning a complex rescue
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery about the luxury of having a few minutes for a life-or-death decision

jokes.text: If only I had two or three minutes to think.
jokes.setup: Saying difficult decision about rescuing Bender
jokes.punchline: Acts like she needs time for what should be an obvious choice
jokes.explanation: Shows the crew's mixed feelings about Bender - obligation vs. genuine care

jokes.text: I can dance like a robot. Will that help?
jokes.setup: Need to act like robots to infiltrate
jokes.punchline: Fry's naive suggestion that robot dancing will help them blend in
jokes.explanation: Good Fry moment showing his childlike approach to serious situations

jokes.text: Fry. First of all, this is serious. Second of all: [does robot dance]
jokes.setup: Leela scolding Fry for not being serious
jokes.punchline: She then does the robot dance herself
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - Leela criticizes then immediately does the same thing

jokes.text: We look stupid. We should've gotten store-bought costumes. / But there's no Woolworth's nearby.
jokes.setup: Complaining about their makeshift robot costumes
jokes.punchline: Fry referencing a long-defunct store chain in the year 3000
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate anachronism from Fry's 20th century perspective

jokes.text: Robot we be. Just two robots out roboting it up.
jokes.setup: Guards asking if they're robots
jokes.punchline: Fry's awkward fake robot speech pattern
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Fry's terrible acting under pressure

jokes.text: Which of the following would you most prefer: A: A puppy? B: A pretty flower from your sweetie? Or C: A large, properly formatted data file?
jokes.setup: Robot identity test
jokes.punchline: Absurdly obvious test contrasting emotional vs. computational preferences
jokes.explanation: Great absurd test - obviously designed to catch humans but hilariously literal

jokes.text: Is the puppy mechanical at all? / No. It's the bad kind of puppy.
jokes.setup: Robot test asking preference between puppy, flower, or data file
jokes.punchline: Fry trying to find a loophole, guard clarifying it's an organic puppy
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist logic - from the robots' perspective, organic life is inherently bad

jokes.text: Come on, Fry. Walk like a robot. / I have to go to the bathroom. / Robots don't have bathrooms. / Right. Where do they smoke in high school?
jokes.setup: Fry needing to use the bathroom while disguised as a robot
jokes.punchline: Fry's bizarre logic connecting robot smoking to bathroom locations
jokes.explanation: Classic Fry non-sequitur showing his strange thought processes

jokes.text: Sir? Are you aware you're leaking coolant at an alarming rate? / Let me patch you up with hot resin.
jokes.setup: Fry urinating while disguised as a robot
jokes.punchline: Helpful robot offering to fix his 'coolant leak' with hot resin
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy with the offer of painful 'help' for a natural function

jokes.text: What sort of robot turns down a free blast of searing-hot resin?
jokes.setup: Fry refusing the resin patch
jokes.punchline: Robot's suspicion at turning down something that would be agony for humans
jokes.explanation: Great robot perspective joke - what's torture to humans is a friendly gesture to robots

jokes.text: Sounds like a romantic evening. I won't keep you.
jokes.setup: Leela saying they have mindless, repetitive tasks to do
jokes.punchline: Robot interpreting boring work as a romantic date
jokes.explanation: Efficient robot culture joke about finding tedium romantic

jokes.text: Say, Wendy? Your chassis's scuffed. Mind if I polish it for you?
jokes.setup: Robot couple at Make-Out Point
jokes.punchline: Robot version of romantic talk involving chassis maintenance
jokes.explanation: Good robot culture worldbuilding with mechanical romantic talk

jokes.text: Relax, Wendy. Humans will never come to our little town. It's perfectly safe to let our guard down.
jokes.setup: Robot girl worried about human sounds
jokes.punchline: Classic dramatic irony as humans are literally hiding nearby
jokes.explanation: Standard dramatic irony setup, functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: I will eat and digest you all with my system of mighty organs!
jokes.setup: Horror movie about humans from robot perspective
jokes.punchline: Describing normal human digestion as a terrifying threat
jokes.explanation: Brilliant perspective flip - normal human biology as monster movie horror

jokes.text: Wow! The 3-D's great! -Mine's not working.
jokes.setup: Watching 3-D movie in disguise
jokes.punchline: Leela can't see 3-D effect due to having one eye
jokes.explanation: Perfect character-based sight gag - her cyclops nature creates practical problem

jokes.text: Yet in the end, he succumbed to a harmless sharpened stick.
jokes.setup: Discussing how the movie human was defeated
jokes.punchline: Robot perspective that a spear is 'harmless' since it can't hurt metal beings
jokes.explanation: Good perspective joke about what's dangerous to different species

jokes.text: Even now, humans lurk in our playgrounds, breezeways... perhaps even our movie theaters!
jokes.setup: Warning about human infiltration
jokes.punchline: Lists movie theaters just as humans are literally in the theater
jokes.explanation: Perfect dramatic irony - warns of exactly what's happening

jokes.text: God help us!
jokes.setup: Robot saying humans lurk in movie theaters
jokes.punchline: Fry's panicked reaction while literally lurking in a movie theater
jokes.explanation: Good tension moment with Fry almost blowing their cover

jokes.text: Too much romance, not enough human killing. It was a real chick flick.
jokes.setup: Asking what robots thought of the human horror movie
jokes.punchline: Dismissing it as a 'chick flick' for having romance instead of violence
jokes.explanation: Clever genre parody that flips expectations about what makes a movie too soft

jokes.text: I heard a human was draining coolant behind garbage can 738.
jokes.setup: Daily human hunt gossip
jokes.punchline: References Fry's earlier urination as coolant leak
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows how rumors spread and distort

jokes.text: I heard they unscrew our lug nuts at night and eat them at brunches.
jokes.setup: Robots sharing rumors about humans during the hunt
jokes.punchline: Absurd fear about humans eating robot parts at fancy meals
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist rumor that parodies urban legends and fears about the other

jokes.text: We have a guest whose irrational hatred for humans makes me look like a human sympathizer.
jokes.setup: Introducing a special guest for the human hunt
jokes.punchline: Setting up Bender as even more extreme than the already genocidal host
jokes.explanation: Good escalation setup for Bender's extreme anti-human persona

jokes.text: Many said I was too extreme when I called for the annihilation of humans, as well as some of the more cunning monkeys.
jokes.setup: Bender describing his extreme anti-human stance
jokes.punchline: Casually including smart monkeys in his genocide plans
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with the casual addition of monkeys to the hit list

jokes.text: And if you enjoyed that diatribe, you'll want to purchase my spoken-word album. Just $18.95.
jokes.setup: Bender finishing his hate speech
jokes.punchline: Immediately pivoting to selling merchandise of his genocide rant
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender character moment - monetizing even his most extreme statements

jokes.text: Bite my shiny metal ass.
jokes.setup: Demonstration of Bender action figure
jokes.punchline: His catchphrase as the toy's main feature
jokes.explanation: Classic catchphrase callback that works as toy merchandise parody

jokes.text: Now, your basic human is between three and 25 feet tall, and is made of a hairy, oily goo in a T-shirt.
jokes.setup: Robot giving hunting tips about humans
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong physical description with bizarre height range
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist description showing robots' complete ignorance about human biology

jokes.text: Nothing. Humans prefer low-calorie bait.
jokes.setup: Checking human traps that are empty
jokes.punchline: Blaming failure on using the wrong diet-conscious bait
jokes.explanation: Good cultural satire about diet culture through robot misunderstanding

jokes.text: But I've got a good feeling about tomorrow.
jokes.setup: 146,000 unsuccessful human hunts in a row
jokes.punchline: Maintaining optimism after an absurd losing streak
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan humor about persistent optimism despite complete failure

jokes.text: Nothing there, except a few shreds of old robot pornography.
jokes.setup: Describing the abandoned adult bookstore
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of robot pornography as debris
jokes.explanation: Robot worldbuilding joke that's mildly amusing but not exceptional

jokes.text: Oh, yeah. You're a bad girl, aren't you?
jokes.setup: Bender looking at robot pornography
jokes.punchline: Bender getting aroused by mechanical imagery
jokes.explanation: Basic character-appropriate innuendo that establishes robot sexuality

jokes.text: They let me go when I said I killed a billion humans.
jokes.setup: Explaining how he got out of trouble
jokes.punchline: Casual claim of massive genocide as a get-out-of-jail card
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy with efficient delivery of extreme claim

jokes.text: I've got wealth, fame, and access to the depths of sleaze from that.
jokes.setup: Explaining why he likes the robot planet
jokes.punchline: Listing pornography access as equal to wealth and fame
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bender's priorities and honesty about his vices

jokes.text: and access to the depths of sleaze from that.
jokes.setup: Listing benefits of staying on robot planet
jokes.punchline: References the robot porn as major life improvement
jokes.explanation: Predictable Bender priorities but consistent with character

jokes.text: Friends? That activates my hilarity unit.
jokes.setup: Fry and Leela calling themselves his friends
jokes.punchline: Robot way of saying something is funny while rejecting friendship
jokes.explanation: Good robot terminology for laughter while being emotionally cold

jokes.text: You're no more my friends than you are with the toaster, phonograph or electric chair.
jokes.setup: Rejecting Fry and Leela as friends
jokes.punchline: Comparing friendship to appliances, escalating to execution device
jokes.explanation: Strong escalation that gets darker with each appliance, ending on electric chair

jokes.text: Come to think of it, I rest my case.
jokes.setup: Prosecutor saying he'll prove humans are guilty of being human
jokes.punchline: Realizing he's already stated his entire case
jokes.explanation: Efficient legal satire about circular reasoning in kangaroo courts

jokes.text: Yeah! He may not have a case, but I am genuinely not a human. / Quiet, human!
jokes.setup: Fry trying to defend himself by claiming non-humanity
jokes.punchline: Judge immediately dismissing him as human anyway
jokes.explanation: Standard courtroom comedy showing the futility of logic in a biased system

jokes.text: He froze up again. Try control-alt-delete. / Jiggle the cord. / Turn him off and on. / Clean the mouse. / Call technical support!
jokes.setup: Robot judge malfunctioning
jokes.punchline: Classic computer troubleshooting steps applied to robot consciousness
jokes.explanation: Relatable computer humor applied to robot consciousness with good escalation

jokes.text: Look, one eye! Count them! One! Not human!
jokes.setup: Leela desperately trying to prove she's not human
jokes.punchline: Using her obvious mutation as evidence while being ignored
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with Leela's desperate but logical argument falling on deaf ears

jokes.text: They'll perform tedious calculations and spot-weld cars until they become obsolete, and given to an inner-city school.
jokes.setup: Describing the sentence for the humans
jokes.punchline: Robot work life ending with being donated to schools like old computers
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary about technology disposal and educational funding

jokes.text: Handcarved from meteorites by robot founders over four centuries ago.
jokes.setup: Describing their ceremonial weapons
jokes.punchline: Absurdly elaborate origin story for simple execution tools
jokes.explanation: Standard parody of ceremonial weapon descriptions, mildly amusing

jokes.text: Plenty of humans have mistreated robots, but not these two. They're my friends.
jokes.setup: Bender being forced to execute his friends
jokes.punchline: Bender's genuine emotional admission of friendship
jokes.explanation: Key character development moment that earns its emotional weight through the setup

jokes.text: Damn right!
jokes.setup: Bender calling humans stupid, putrid cowards
jokes.punchline: Fry's enthusiastic agreement with the insult about himself
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Fry's self-deprecating nature and total lack of self-awareness

jokes.text: We use them as scapegoats to distract the public from their real problems. / Like our crippling lug nut shortage. / And a corrupt government of robot elders. / That's for sure. / Quiet, Jimmy.
jokes.setup: Robot elders revealing their scapegoating strategy
jokes.punchline: Accidentally revealing actual corruption while trying to explain fake problems
jokes.explanation: Excellent political satire that works on multiple levels about scapegoating and corruption

jokes.text: He'll do it! He's crazy! / Did we make that up? / Gee, I can't remember anymore. / It might be from that stupid movie.
jokes.setup: Bender threatening to breathe fire
jokes.punchline: Elders can't remember if robots can actually breathe fire or if it's movie fiction
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about propaganda creating false beliefs even among the propagandists

jokes.text: He'll do it! He's crazy! -Did we make that up? -Gee, I can't remember anymore.
jokes.setup: Bender threatening to breathe fire
jokes.punchline: Leaders confused about their own propaganda
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about propaganda creators forgetting what's true

jokes.text: Lug nuts! Precious lug nuts!
jokes.setup: The package delivery reveals lug nuts
jokes.punchline: The supposedly fake problem was actually real and desperately needed
jokes.explanation: Great payoff that recontextualizes earlier 'fake' problem as genuinely serious

jokes.text: Thanks. You know I made Robanukah up to get out of work, right? / Of course. / That doesn't make it less meaningful.
jokes.setup: Bender admitting Robanukah was fake
jokes.punchline: Friends accepting the fake holiday as meaningful anyway
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment that subverts cynicism - fake traditions can still have meaning

jokes.text: Thanks. You know I made Robanukah up to get out of work, right? -Of course.
jokes.setup: Celebrating Robanukah with Bender
jokes.punchline: Bender admits the holiday was fake, Leela already knew
jokes.explanation: Good callback that resolves the fake holiday subplot with character insight

jokes.text: Hey, you guys are good. How the hell do you do that?
jokes.setup: Fry and Leela doing traditional Jewish dance moves for Robanukah
jokes.punchline: Bender amazed at their ability to do dances from his made-up holiday
jokes.explanation: Good payoff showing the irony that real dances work for fake holidays
